title: trisetum flavescens subsp. corsicum
intro: Trisetum flavescens subsp. corsicum, also known as Corsican hair-grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The plant is said to have several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the relief of menstrual cramps. Trisetum flavescens subsp. corsicum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is often used in traditional medicine to address various health concerns.
